title: Add worktree serialization short-circuit to _beads_conflict and _find_conflict_reason
description: In `src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py`, add a short-circuit at the top of the same-feature-tree branch of `_beads_conflict()` (around line 421): when `config.scheduler.serialize_within_feature_tree` is `True`, both beads are mutating agents (`developer`, `tester`, `documentation`), and they share a feature tree, return `True` immediately. Also add a matching early-return in `_find_conflict_reason()` (around line 357) that returns `"worktree serialization enabled — waiting on in-progress {active.bead_id}"` before existing checks when the same three conditions hold. Use the existing `MUTATING_AGENTS` constant already in scope — no new imports needed.
acceptance_criteria: With flag `False`, `_beads_conflict()` behaviour is unchanged for all bead pairs
With flag `True`, two mutating beads in the same feature tree with non-overlapping `expected_files` return `True` from `_beads_conflict()`
With flag `True`, two mutating beads in different feature trees return `False` from `_beads_conflict()`
With flag `True`, a non-mutating bead (planner/review/recovery) paired with a mutating bead in the same tree returns `False` from `_beads_conflict()`
`_find_conflict_reason()` returns `"worktree serialization enabled — waiting on in-progress <bead_id>"` when the new rule fires
New reason string precedes existing checks in `_find_conflict_reason()`

handoff_summary.completed: Inserted `if self.config.scheduler.serialize_within_feature_tree: return True` at the top of the same-feature-tree mutating-agent block in `_beads_conflict()` (line 431). Added a preceding guard in `_find_conflict_reason()` (lines 366-372) that returns `"worktree serialization enabled — waiting on in-progress {active.bead_id}"` when the flag is set, both beads are in the same feature tree, and both are mutating agents. Both checks use the existing `MUTATING_AGENTS` constant already in scope — no new imports needed.
handoff_summary.risks: None. The new code paths only activate when `serialize_within_feature_tree=True`; the flag defaults to `False`, so all existing behaviour is unchanged for the default case.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify the six acceptance criteria: flag=False leaves existing behaviour unchanged; flag=True causes two mutating beads in the same tree with non-overlapping expected_files to return True from _beads_conflict; two mutating beads in different trees still return False; a non-mutating bead paired with a mutating bead in the same tree returns False; _find_conflict_reason returns the new serialization reason string; and the new reason precedes the existing checks.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: The short-circuit is placed inside the existing `if same_feature_tree and bead.agent_type in MUTATING_AGENTS and active.agent_type in MUTATING_AGENTS:` block rather than as a separate top-level guard. This avoids duplicating the condition and makes the intent clear: when the flag is set, we skip the scope-presence check and return True immediately, serialising all mutating agents in the same worktree regardless of whether they have declared file scopes. The `_find_conflict_reason()` guard is placed after `same_feature_tree` is computed (reusing it) and before the existing `"worktree in use"` and `"file-scope conflict"` messages, ensuring the new reason string is emitted first whenever the serialisation rule fires.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should cover: (1) flag=False, two mutating beads same tree with overlapping files — existing conflict; (2) flag=False, two mutating beads same tree with non-overlapping files — no conflict (unchanged); (3) flag=True, two mutating beads same tree with non-overlapping files — conflict (new behaviour); (4) flag=True, two mutating beads different trees — no conflict; (5) flag=True, one non-mutating (planner/review/recovery) + one mutating same tree — no conflict; (6) _find_conflict_reason returns the exact string "worktree serialization enabled — waiting on in-progress {bead_id}" when the rule fires.

execution_history.summary: Added `serialize_within_feature_tree` short-circuit to `_beads_conflict()` and matching early-return to `_find_conflict_reason()` in `src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
